CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-19-2022 Set For Trial On Jan-30-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Petition To Compel Arbitration And For An Order To Stay The Pending Litigation (Motions)
Off Calendar May-17-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Attys. CATHERINE PASTRIKOS KELLY, Esq. of Meyner and Landis LLP (Tel: 973-602-3423) and JAIME G. TOUCHSTONE, Esq. of Futterman Dupree Dodd Croley Maier LLP (Tel: 415-399-3840) for plaintiff;
Atty. ALLISON L. CARDENAS, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (Tel: 415-362-2580) for defendants Conopco, Inc. d/b/a Unilever.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT TATCHA LLC, CONOPCO, INC., D/B/A UNILEVER's Petition To Compel Arbitration And For An Order To Stay The Pending Litigation.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' motion to compel arbitration is denied on waiver grounds.

In the nine months since plaintiff filed this case: defendants filed answers; after meeting and conferring, defendants filed amended answers; plaintiff deposited jury fees and demanded a jury trial; the parties filed multiple case management statements; plaintiff served document requests, interrogatories and requests for admission; plaintiff agreed to three extensions for defendants' discovery responses; the parties agreed to and filed a joint protective order regarding confidentiality; defendants responded and objected to plaintiff's discovery. (Opp. 8:18-9:1.)

Thus, "the parties' actions are inconsistence with the right to arbitrate;" "the litigation machinery has been substantially invoked;" defendants "delayed for a long period of time" before petitioning for arbitration; "important intervening steps" such as discovery have "taken place;" and "the delay affected, misled, or prejudiced" plaintiff. (St. Agnes Medical Center v. PacificCare of California (2003) 31 Cal.4th 1187, 1196 (internal quote marks deleted).)) Plaintiff's other grounds need not be reached.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF FRANCOIS BONIN'S MOTION TO ADMIT ADAM N. LEVITSKY AS COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

No appearances are made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The verified application of Adam N. Levitsky to appear pro hac vice as counsel to plaintiff is granted. No opposition filed, and the application conforms to Cal. R. Ct. 9.40.

Order signed.
